questions.text:
A student has an electronic calculator. The calculator has a screen that displays the result of calculations. The calculator has 8-bit registers.

State the highest denary number that could be stored in a single 8-bit register.

A student has an electronic calculator. The calculator has a screen that displays the result of calculations. The calculator has 8-bit registers.

Denary numbers are typed into the calculator. They are converted to binary numbers and stored in the 8-bit registers. The student types in the denary number 23 and the denary number 168.

Give the 8-bit binary numbers that are stored in the registers for these two denary numbers.

23
168

A student has an electronic calculator. The calculator has a screen that displays the result of calculations. The calculator has 8-bit registers.

The result of a calculation that is stored in a register is the binary number 00001011. Give the denary number that will be displayed on the screen for this binary number.

00001011

A student has an electronic calculator. The calculator has a screen that displays the result of calculations. The calculator has 8-bit registers.

Another result of a calculation that is stored in a register is the binary number 01000010. Give the denary number that will be displayed on the screen for this binary number.

01000010

A student has an electronic calculator. The calculator has a screen that displays the result of calculations. The calculator has 8-bit registers.

Two binary numbers stored in the registers are 01110110 and 00110000. Add the binary numbers using binary addition. Show all your working. Give your answer in binary.

  0 1 1 1 0 1 1 0
+ 0 0 1 1 0 0 0 0

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct nibble. One mark for a correct method of working, for example carries.

marking_points.text: Correct carries shown (e.g. 1 1 1 above appropriate columns)
Correct upper nibble of result: 1010
Correct lower nibble of result: 0110 (full answer: 10100110)

questions.text:
A student has an electronic calculator. The calculator has a screen that displays the result of calculations. The calculator has 8-bit registers.

The result of a calculation could be a negative denary number. State how the calculator could represent a negative denary number as a binary number.

marking_points.text: Using two's complement

questions.text:
A student has an electronic calculator. The calculator has a screen that displays the result of calculations. The calculator has 8-bit registers.

The electronic calculator is an example of an embedded system. Explain why it is an example of an embedded system.

marking_points.text: It has a single/limited function
It has dedicated hardware
It has a microprocessor
Its function is not easily changed

questions.text:
An employee of a company sells houses. He sends images of the houses to his company.

The employee compresses the image files using lossy compression before sending them. Lossy compression reduces the size of the image files.

Give two other reductions that lossy compression makes to the images.

1
2

marking_points.text: Resolution // number of pixels
Colour depth // bit depth
Quality // detail
Metadata

questions.text:
An employee of a company sells houses. He sends images of the houses to his company.

The image data is broken down into packets to be sent across a network to the company. The destination internet protocol (IP) address is read by a device in the network. The device uses the IP address to forward the packet towards its correct destination.

State the name of the device that reads the destination IP address.

marking_points.text: Router

questions.text:
An employee of a company sells houses. He sends images of the houses to his company.

The image data is broken down into packets to be sent across a network to the company. The destination internet protocol (IP) address is read by a device in the network. The device uses the IP address to forward the packet towards its correct destination.

Identify the part of the packet where the destination IP address is found.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: (Packet) header

questions.text:
An employee of a company sells houses. He sends images of the houses to his company.

The IP address has an IPv6 format. Give three characteristics of the IPv6 format.

1
2
3
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

marking_points.text: It has 128 bits
Hexadecimal
Separated by colons
8 groups of digits
Each group is 4 digits
Consecutive groups of 0s can be represented by :: once

questions.text:
An employee of a company sells houses. He sends images of the houses to his company.

An echo check is used to check for errors after the image has been transmitted. Draw and annotate a diagram to show how the echo check would detect any errors.

visuals.description: Diagram showing sender transmitting data to receiver, receiver sending copy back to sender, sender comparing original data with copy, error detected if they do not match

marking_points.text: (Data being sent to the receiver) and a copy sent back from the receiver to the sender
Sending device comparing the original data and the copy
If original data and copy don't match an error is detected

questions.text:
An employee of a company sells houses. He sends images of the houses to his company.

The image data is encrypted using symmetric encryption. Tick (✓) one box to show the correct statement about symmetric encryption.

A Encrypted text is known as plain text.
B The most secure form of encryption.
C The encryption key is shared with any device receiving the encrypted data.
D Two different keys are used.

rows: A Encrypted text is known as plain text.
B The most secure form of encryption.
C The encryption key is shared with any device receiving the encrypted data.
D Two different keys are used.

marking_points.text: C

questions.text:
An employee of a company sells houses. He sends images of the houses to his company.

The image data is encrypted using symmetric encryption. Give one other type of encryption that could be used.

marking_points.text: Asymmetric

questions.text:
A student has a portable device. He uses an on-screen keyboard to write messages to his friend.

Explain how the text the student writes is converted to binary to be processed.

marking_points.text: A character set is used
… such as ASCII/Unicode
Each character has a unique binary value that it is converted to

questions.text:
A student has a portable device. He uses an on-screen keyboard to write messages to his friend.

The device has 4 gibibytes (GiB) of random access memory (RAM). State the amount of RAM the device has in mebibytes (MiB).

A student has a portable device. He uses an on-screen keyboard to write messages to his friend.

The device has 4 gibibytes (GiB) of random access memory (RAM). State the amount of RAM the device has in tebibytes (TiB). Your answer can be given rounded to 3 decimal places.

A student has a portable device. He uses an on-screen keyboard to write messages to his friend.

Explain why the portable device needs RAM.

marking_points.text: To store data/instructions that are currently in use
To store software/programs that are currently in use
For volatile storage // to store data temporarily
To allow data to be accessed directly by the CPU/processor // To allow data to be stored closer to the CPU/processor

questions.text:
A student has a portable device. He uses an on-screen keyboard to write messages to his friend.

The device has a fingerprint scanner to allow the student to unlock the portable device. Circle only three terms that can be used to describe the fingerprint scanner.

authentication  biometric  malware  parity check
firewall  input device  output device  storage device

marking_points.text: Authentication
Biometric
Input device

questions.text:
A computer game development company are reviewing their current security solutions. The company wants employees to be aware of how they can identify phishing.

Describe the process of phishing.

marking_points.text: A legitimate looking email is sent to the user
The user clicks a link in the email // the user replies to the email
The user is directed to a fake website
The user enters their personal data …
… that is stolen/used by a third party (for malicious purposes)

questions.text:
A computer game development company are reviewing their current security solutions.

Give two ways an employee could identify phishing.

1
2

marking_points.text: Check the email address that the email has been sent from
Check the tone/spelling of the email
Check the URL that is attached to the link

questions.text:
A computer game development company are reviewing their current security solutions.

The company has installed anti-malware software on all employees' computers. Give three examples of malware that anti-malware software can detect.

1
2
3

marking_points.text: Virus
Worm
Trojan horse
Spyware // keylogger
Ransomware
Adware

questions.text:
A computer game development company are reviewing their current security solutions.

The computer game developers use a high-level language to create a computer game. State what is meant by a high-level language.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: A language that uses English-like statements
It is a language that needs to be converted to machine code (to be processed by a computer)
It is a portable language (that can be used on any computer)

questions.text:
A computer game development company are reviewing their current security solutions.

The computer game developers use a high-level language to create a computer game. One benefit of using a high-level language is that the code is easy to debug. Give two other benefits of using a high-level language.

1
2
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Easy/quick to read/write/understand
Machine independent
Less likely to make errors
Programmer can focus on the problem instead of the manipulation of memory/hardware

questions.text:
A computer game development company are reviewing their current security solutions.

The computer game developers use a high-level language to create a computer game. One drawback of using a high-level language is that it must be converted to a low-level language before it can be run. Give one other drawback of using a high-level language.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Cannot directly manipulate the hardware
Takes longer to convert (than low level-language)
Cannot use specialised hardware
Uses more memory // less efficient memory use

questions.text:
A computer game development company are reviewing their current security solutions.

The computer game developers use a high-level language to create a computer game. The computer game developers use an interpreter instead of a compiler when developing the computer game. Explain why an interpreter would be more appropriate for developing the computer game.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: The code is translated and executed line by line
… stopping when an error is found (showing the error location)
… meaning errors can be corrected in real time
No need to retranslate each time an error is fixed
… making it easier to debug
Sections of code can be easily tested
… meaning the whole program doesn't need to be written for testing

questions.text:
A supermarket wants to start delivering groceries to local elderly people, using robots.

Describe the characteristics of a robot.

marking_points.text: A mechanical structure/framework
Electrical components
… such as sensors/microprocessor/actuators
Programmable
Has the ability to move

questions.text:
A supermarket wants to start delivering groceries to local elderly people, using robots.

Explain one benefit to the supermarket of using robots to deliver groceries to elderly customers.

answers.scoring_rule: Two from (one for benefit and one for matching explanation):

marking_points.text: Supermarket may gain more customers
… as elderly people who could not previously make it to the store can now shop there // … which will increase their sales/profits
Supermarket can serve customers more efficiently
… as multiple orders can be delivered at the same time

questions.text:
A supermarket wants to start delivering groceries to local elderly people, using robots.

Explain one drawback to the elderly customers of robots being used to deliver their groceries.

answers.scoring_rule: Two from (one for drawback and one for matching explanation):

marking_points.text: They may get less exercise
… as they no longer need to walk to/round the store to get their shopping // … that could negatively affect their health
They may struggle to use technology // may mistrust technology // would prefer an employee/person to deliver it
… and this may cause additional stress/anxiety // … leading to making mistakes in receiving the delivery

questions.text:
A supermarket wants to start delivering groceries to local elderly people, using robots.

Each robot has a media access control (MAC) address. Tick (✓) one box to show the correct statement about a MAC address.

A It is assigned by the manufacturer.
B It is assigned by the product.
C It is assigned by the router.
D It is assigned by the user.

rows: A It is assigned by the manufacturer.
B It is assigned by the product.
C It is assigned by the router.
D It is assigned by the user.

marking_points.text: A

questions.text:
A supermarket wants to start delivering groceries to local elderly people, using robots.

The robots have artificial intelligence (AI). Give three ways the robots could make use of AI.

1
2
3

marking_points.text: It could learn the route to a customer's house
It could learn where there are obstacles on a route
It could learn to deal with obstacles more effectively
It could reason which parts of the route are paths and which are roads
It can use machine learning
To identify patterns in the route data

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU).

Complete the statements about the operation of the CPU. Use the terms from the list. Not all terms will need to be used. You should only use each term once.

accumulator  arithmetic logic unit  character set
control unit  current instruction register  cipher decoding set
instruction counter  instruction set  memory address register
memory data register  memory instruction register  next instruction register
program counter  program register

The [blank] stores the address of the next instruction to be fetched. This address is then sent to the [blank].

This address is the location in RAM where the instruction can be found. The instruction is retrieved from RAM and immediately stored in the [blank].

The instruction is then sent to the [blank] [blank] where it is decoded by the [blank] [blank]. The instruction is decoded using an [blank].

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct term in the correct place.

marking_points.text: program counter (stores the address of the next instruction to be processed)
memory address register (This address is then sent to the …)
memory data register (The instruction is retrieved from RAM and immediately stored in the …)
current instruction register (The instruction is then sent to the …)
control unit (where it is decoded by the …)
instruction set (The instruction is decoded using an …)

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU).

Handling interrupts is one function of an operating system. An interrupt is sent to the CPU every time a key is pressed on the keyboard. Give the name of this type of interrupt.

marking_points.text: Hardware (interrupt)

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU).

Handling interrupts is one function of an operating system. Give the name of the program that services the interrupt.

marking_points.text: Interrupt handler // Interrupt service routine

questions.text:
A computer with a Von Neumann architecture has a central processing unit (CPU).

Handling interrupts is one function of an operating system. Give one other function of an operating system.

marking_points.text: managing files
providing an interface
managing peripherals and drivers
managing memory
managing multitasking
providing a platform for running applications
providing system security
managing user accounts

questions.text:
A theatre has a microphone on stage that is connected by cables to several speakers around the theatre. A person speaks into the microphone and their voice is output from the speakers.

Identify the most appropriate data transmission method for this purpose. Explain your choice.

Data transmission method
Explanation
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct data transmission method.

marking_points.text: Serial
Simplex
Serial: Data will need to travel long distances
Serial: One wire is used // bits are sent in order // sent one bit at a time
Serial: Less chance of data being skewed // less chance of error
Serial: Transmission speed would be adequate
Simplex: Data only needs to be sent in one direction
Simplex: Data does not need to be sent back from the speakers to the microphone
